subject: Public Health
title: Alcohol and the Liver
description: A micro-lesson that makes Alcohol and the Liver usable.

hook.text: The liver processes every drink you take—and heavy drinking scars it beyond repair, leading to cirrhosis and death.

buildup.text: Alcohol is metabolized into acetaldehyde—a toxic compound that damages liver cells with each exposure.

discovery.text: Fatty liver develops first, then inflammation, then fibrosis, and finally cirrhosis—a progression that takes years.

twist.text: Cirrhosis is irreversible—once scar tissue replaces healthy liver, the only cure is transplantation.

climax.text: Stopping alcohol at any stage before cirrhosis allows the liver to partially regenerate—earlier is always better.

punchline.text: The liver forgives a lot—but not forever. Every drink leaves a mark.

quiz.question: What is the toxic compound produced when the liver processes alcohol?
